characters_mentioned: researchers from the Soviet Academy of Sciences

relationships_established.text: Nikola Tesla considered the pigeons his friends and fed them.
Sava Kosanovic was Nikola Tesla's nephew and was privy to some of Tesla's secrets.
Nikola Tesla created a small ledger, which he considered very important and secretive, containing 'c
Tesla entrusted Kosanovic with a secret ledger and made him promise to destroy it for protection.
Father Toma recorded Nikola Tesla's baptismal entry.
Milutin is Nikola Tesla's father.
Duka is Nikola Tesla's mother.
Marta is the midwife attending Duka's birth; Duka's strange perceptions are witnessed by Marta.
A grandmother writes a letter to her grandson who is in the Austrian army.
Dane uniquely observed and understood Nikola's true nature, acting as a steadfast observer.
Thomas Edison's working method is characterized by a struggle to dominate or control nature.
Tesla perceives himself as connected to a mysterious 'place he came from'.
Tesla built a magnifying transmitter.
Tesla ordered his assistant to activate the magnifying transmitter.
Tesla possessed and likely created (or heavily annotated) the Black Notebook, a crucial personal art
The patent office rejected Tesla's patent, acting as an antagonist to his unconventional inventions.
Tesla and Marconi were historical rivals, particularly in the race for radio technology and associat
Tesla developed and possessed prototypes of an 'automated oscillator' which was destroyed in the fir
Tesla created shadowgraphs, a form of early X-ray imaging, which were lost in the fire.
The night watchman witnessed the lab fire and was profoundly traumatized by its unusual and terrifyi

open_mysteries_introduced: Was it taken before they arrived? Did someone else know what Tesla had been documenting? Or did the ledger simply... leave?
Why did the agents arrive so fast?
Who was watching that hotel room, waiting for the old man's heart to stop beating so they could take what he knew?
But what if they were looking for a receipt?
What if they were looking for the documentation of a crime that happened eighty-six years earlier? A crime against nature itself.
What kind of palsy took Father Toma's hand for such a short time, then released it?
So why does the legend persist? Why did the Tesla family tell the story of the storm for generations?
Was he trying to drown the frequency?
sample_lore_facts: But someone was already disturbing. Within hours, before the family was notified, before the obituaries were written, before the world even knew that Nikola Tesla was gone, the agents were there.
They hauled out eighty trunks. Eighty trunks containing the lifetime work of the man who invented the twentieth century.
Kosanovic told the agents that something was missing. Not the scientific notebooks. Not the famous journals. Something else. A small ledger, barely larger than a man's hand. Tesla had shown it to him

Eighty trunks left the Hotel New Yorker. Only sixty arrived at the museum in Belgrade years later. Twenty trunks vanished. One quarter of his life's work. Gone. Vaporized into the dark machinery of go
sample_historical_facts: The agent in charge was P.E. Foxworth, assistant director of the New York FBI office.
They found the black notebooks filled with calculations. They found the journals from Colorado Springs. They found letters, patents, photographs.
It was January seventh, nineteen forty-three. Room thirty-three twenty-seven of the Hotel New Yorker. On the thirty-third floor, an eighty-six-year-old man lay dead in the bed.
The agents were from the Office of Alien Property. A wartime agency designed to seize the assets of enemies of the state.
sample_sensory_language: His frame was wasted away to skin and bone, his eyes sunken, his chest finally still.
The body wasn't even cold yet.
They swept through the two small rooms like locusts.
Vaporized into the dark machinery of government.
carved into the grain with a force that almost tore the page
grey, watery separation that smelled of ozone
The birds have forgotten us. The sky is empty. Even the flies do not land on the meat.
No bird song. No insect hum. Just the sound of the wind moving through the dry grass, and the sound
